problem.title: Count Complete Tree Nodes
problem.description: Given the root of a complete binary tree, return the number of the nodes in the tree.
assumptions: Tree is a complete binary tree (all levels fully filled except possibly the last, filled left to right).

example.explanation: Count all nodes. Use binary search on last level for O(log²n) solution.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Exploit the complete-tree property by comparing the left-spine height and right-spine height at each node. If they are equal, the left subtree is a perfect binary tree with 2^h nodes, so recurse only into the right subtree. Otherwise, the right subtree is a perfect tree of height h-1, and we recurse into the left subtree. This reduces the problem size by half each step, yielding O(log^2 n) time.
